summary: ANDROID 🟥 | FMD | Play/Pause button giving different outcomes
scenario: Find My Device tone fails to play after Stop then Play, and rapid Play presses cause audio stacking and Pause button disappearance on Poly device
description_clean: In the Find My Device (FMD) feature, pressing the Play/Pause button on a connected Poly device produces inconsistent results. When pressing Play then Stop then Play again, no tone is heard. When pressing Play quickly multiple times, tones can stack into very bad noise, and the Pause button disappears, requiring the device to be turned off. Expected behavior is that the tone plays properly each time.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Find My Device (FMD).
4. Tap the Play button to start the FMD tone.
5. Tap the Stop button to stop the FMD tone.
6. Tap the Play button again to replay the FMD tone.
7. Observe whether the tone is heard.
8. Tap the Play button quickly multiple times in succession.
9. Observe the audio output and the UI state of the Play/Pause button.
expected: The FMD tone plays properly each time Play is pressed. Pressing Stop then Play again replays the tone. Rapid presses do not cause audio stacking or UI corruption; the Play/Pause button remains functional.
actual: When pressing Play then Stop then Play again, no tone is heard. When pressing Play quickly multiple times, tones stack into very bad noise, and the Pause button disappears, requiring the device to be powered off.
